name: John Carisi
abstract: John E. Carisi (February 23, 1922 – October 3, 1992) was an American trumpeter and composer.

works_created.t: Yamaha Suite for Trumpet and Saxophone Quartet

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder
about_quotes.evidence: we had one called – we didn’t use the title, but it was called The Vulgar Bulgar, by Johnny Carisi. That’s what we used to open up with.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: for the first part of the tour, for the first couple of weeks, we had we had arrangements by Tad Dameron, by John Carisi, by Bob Prince
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: Subsequently Harvey then had another piece written [for tuba and string quartet] by Johnny Carisi, the guy who wrote Israel.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Sauter
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] John Carisi studied-- [INTERVIEWEE] John came from McKinley's band; he had been up at Yale with Miller...

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: I became very friendly and closely associated with the activities of ... Johnny Carisi.
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: we were all there... John Carisi... George Russell was another one that was down there all the time... we were all there, that’s who was there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
